clusters.neutral_headline: Zohran Mamdani's chosen candidates take home wins
bullet_summary: Bill Cassidy of Louisiana said he lost his temper and Trump raised his voice, but the president said, "We have a really well unified party
Reported by CBS News, France24, Financial Times and others

items.title: Zohran Mamdani's chosen candidates take home wins
items.standfirst: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three far-left House candidates, and all three won their primaries Tuesday, raising questions about the future of the Democratic Party. Trump-backed candidates also had a good showing. Ed O'Keefe reports.
items.content: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three far-left House candidates, and all three won their primaries Tuesday, raising questions about the future of the Democratic Party. Trump-backed candidates also had a good showing. Ed O'Keefe reports.

items.title: Trump's luncheon with GOP senators devolves into shouting match
items.standfirst: President Trump threw Capitol Hill into turmoil on Wednesday, abruptly canceling plans to sign a bill aimed at lowering housing costs. His closed-door luncheon with Republican senators turned into a shouting match over the war in Iran. The president wants senators to pass a different bill that would change voting rules. Caitlin Huey Burns reports.
items.content: President Trump threw Capitol Hill into turmoil on Wednesday, abruptly canceling plans to sign a bill aimed at lowering housing costs. His closed-door luncheon with Republican senators turned into a shouting match over the war in Iran. The president wants senators to pass a different bill that would change voting rules. Caitlin Huey Burns reports.

items.title: Democratic, Republican strategists react to progressive wins in New York primaries
items.standfirst: What do Tuesday night's victories from NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani's endorsed candidates say about the future of the Democratic Party? Political strategists Ameshia Cross and Tricia McLaughlin join with analysis.
items.content: What do Tuesday night's victories from NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani's endorsed candidates say about the future of the Democratic Party? Political strategists Ameshia Cross and Tricia McLaughlin join with analysis.

items.title: How Democratic Socialist primary wins in New York could shape upcoming elections
items.standfirst: New York City Democratic Socialist Mayor Zohran Mamdani flexed his political muscle on Tuesday, with all three of his endorsed political candidates winning their House primaries. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more.
items.content: New York City Democratic Socialist Mayor Zohran Mamdani flexed his political muscle on Tuesday, with all three of his endorsed political candidates winning their House primaries. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more.

items.title: Examining Trump's decision to cancel bipartisan housing bill signing
items.standfirst: President Trump on Wednesday canceled plans to sign a bipartisan housing bill, demanding that lawmakers first pass the SAVE Act. Political strategists Ameshia Cross and Tricia McLaughlin join with analysis.
items.content: President Trump on Wednesday canceled plans to sign a bipartisan housing bill, demanding that lawmakers first pass the SAVE Act. Political strategists Ameshia Cross and Tricia McLaughlin join with analysis.

items.title: Trump and Cassidy have spat in testy meeting, sources say
items.standfirst: President Trump had a testy meeting with Senate Republicans on Wednesday. Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana said he lost his temper and Trump raised his voice, but the president said, "We have a really well unified party." CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.
items.content: President Trump had a testy meeting with Senate Republicans on Wednesday. Sen. Bill Cassidy of Louisiana said he lost his temper and Trump raised his voice, but the president said, "We have a really well unified party." CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.

items.title: Are Democrats having a Tea Party moment?
items.standfirst: Progressive candidates endorsed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani emerged victorious in Tuesday's primaries. CBS News' Fin Gómez and Anthony Salvanto join with analysis.
items.content: Progressive candidates endorsed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani emerged victorious in Tuesday's primaries. CBS News' Fin Gómez and Anthony Salvanto join with analysis.

items.title: Trump and Cassidy had testy exchange during meeting with GOP senators, sources say
items.standfirst: President Trump had a testy meeting with Republican senators focused mostly on the Iran war, sources say. CBS News' Taurean Small and Nikole Killion have the latest.
items.content: President Trump had a testy meeting with Republican senators focused mostly on the Iran war, sources say. CBS News' Taurean Small and Nikole Killion have the latest.

items.title: Takeaways from wins for Mamdani-backed candidates
items.standfirst: All three candidates endorsed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani won their primary races on Tuesday. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more on what this means nationally.
items.content: All three candidates endorsed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani won their primary races on Tuesday. CBS News political reporter Zak Hudak has more on what this means nationally.

items.title: Trump cancels housing bill signing, reiterates demand for SAVE America Act
items.standfirst: President Trump canceled a planned signing ceremony on Wednesday for a housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins.
items.content: President Trump canceled a planned signing ceremony on Wednesday for a housing affordability bill that passed Congress by wide bipartisan margins.

items.title: Trump pressures congressional Republicans by delaying landmark housing bill over voter ID push
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump announced Wednesday that he would not sign a new landmark housing bill until congressional Repulicans pass his proposed SAVE America Act to restrict voting procedures which he has been calling for for months.
items.content: US President Donald Trump announced Wednesday that he would not sign a new landmark housing bill until congressional Repulicans pass his proposed SAVE America Act to restrict voting procedures which he has been calling for for months.

items.title: Congress passes major housing bill. And, Mamdani-backed candidates sweep NYC primaries
items.standfirst: Congress has passed the largest housing affordability bill in decades. And all three candidates endorsed by New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani have won their primaries.
items.content: Congress has passed the largest housing affordability bill in decades. And all three candidates endorsed by New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani have won their primaries. (Image credit: Heather Diehl)

items.title: Trump says NATO allies ‘let down’ US by not backing Iran war
items.standfirst: Trump told NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday he was disappointed that alliance members did not back the US military campaign against Iran, saying Washington had "demolished" Iran without needing assistance. Rutte defended allies, noting that 4,000-5,000 US aircraft operated from bases in Europe during the conflict.
items.content: Trump told NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday he was disappointed that alliance members did not back the US military campaign against Iran, saying Washington had "demolished" Iran without needing assistance. Rutte defended allies, noting that 4,000-5,000 US aircraft operated from bases in Europe during the conflict.

items.title: US in 'panic' at prospect of Gulf States going it alone, analyst says
items.standfirst: Secretary of State Marco Rubio promised the United States' Gulf allies that Washington would protect their interests as it seeks to hammer out a final settlement of the Middle East war in talks with Iran. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, independent journalist and author of the newsletter Badlands Borzou Daraghai says that the White House is experiencing "panic" at the prospect of Gulf countries creating an alternative security arrangement in the region that could undermine the Abraham accords.

items.title: France experiences its hottest day since measurements began in 1947
items.standfirst: France on Wednesday experienced its hottest day since measurements began in 1947, the national weather agency said, breaking a record set just a day earlier. Around 44 million of the nearly 67 million people in France are currently under the highest red alert level for heat. FRANCE 24's Luke Shrago reports.

clusters.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years

bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
items.standfirst: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western provincePakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west.The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Afghan border, the district police chief, Waqar Ahmad, said. Continue reading...
items.content: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western provincePakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west.The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Afghan border, the district police chief, Waqar Ahmad, said. items.title: Great Britain’s grid operator pays £10m for extra power to avoid supply crunch tonight
items.standfirst: Neso expected to pay millions to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on fans and air conditioningGreat Britain’s grid operator is expected to pay millions to fire up gas power plants to avoid a rare summer power supply crunch on Wednesday evening as extreme heat puts pressure on the energy system.The National Energy System Operator (Neso) is expected to pay about £10m on Wednesday to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on air conditioners and electric fans, according to industry data. Continue reading...
items.content: Neso expected to pay millions to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on fans and air conditioningGreat Britain’s grid operator is expected to pay millions to fire up gas power plants to avoid a rare summer power supply crunch on Wednesday evening as extreme heat puts pressure on the energy system.The National Energy System Operator (Neso) is expected to pay about £10m on Wednesday to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on air conditioners and electric fans, according to industry data. Continue reading...

items.title: Europe’s heatwave drives electricity prices to new highs as demand soars
items.standfirst: Great Britain has paid at least six times the normal price for imported power as millions turn on air conditioning and windfarm output sagsThe heatwave has prompted a sharp rise in electricity prices across European markets as millions turn to air conditioners and electric fans to battle record high temperatures, which have also caused a string of power plant outages across the continent.Great Britain imported electricity from Europe at more than six times the normal price on Tuesday as the high-pressure heat dome has slowed wind speeds, hitting renewable energy generation, and led to outages at multiple gas plants across the country. Continue reading...
items.content: Great Britain has paid at least six times the normal price for imported power as millions turn on air conditioning and windfarm output sagsThe heatwave has prompted a sharp rise in electricity prices across European markets as millions turn to air conditioners and electric fans to battle record high temperatures, which have also caused a string of power plant outages across the continent.Great Britain imported electricity from Europe at more than six times the normal price on Tuesday as the high-pressure heat dome has slowed wind speeds, hitting renewable energy generation, and led to outages at multiple gas plants across the country. clusters.title: Trump says he’s ordered investigation into oil companies over alleged price gouging
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump accuses oil companies of gas price ‘gouging,’ calls for DOJ probe
bullet_summary: “The big Oil Companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for Oil,” the president said
Reported by Guardian Business, NBC World

items.title: Trump says he’s ordered investigation into oil companies over alleged price gouging
items.standfirst: Trump says he instructed justice department to investigate oil firms over high gas prices amid Middle East conflictDonald Trump said on Wednesday that he had instructed the US Department of Justice to investigate oil companies for alleged price gouging, accusing them of not lowering gas prices enough amid conflict in the Middle East.“The big oil companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for oil. Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being ‘gouged.’ I have instructed the DOJ to immediately start looking into this,” Trump wrote in a social media post late on Tuesday night. “Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Continue reading...
items.content: Trump says he instructed justice department to investigate oil firms over high gas prices amid Middle East conflictDonald Trump said on Wednesday that he had instructed the US Department of Justice to investigate oil companies for alleged price gouging, accusing them of not lowering gas prices enough amid conflict in the Middle East.“The big oil companies are not dropping their price at the pump commensurate with the sharply lower prices they are paying for oil. Those prices are dropping like a rock! In other words, customers are being ‘gouged.’ I have instructed the DOJ to immediately start looking into this,” Trump wrote in a social media post late on Tuesday night. “Gasoline prices better start going down a lot faster than what I’m seeing!” Continue reading...
